name: scikit-network (Graph Analysis with Scikit-learn API)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); Telecom Paris / Thomas Bonald

performance.notes: SciPy sparse matrices; fast for million-node graphs with sparse structure

agent_readiness.notes: Scikit-learn-style graph analysis on SciPy sparse matrices. from sknetwork.clustering import Louvain, Leiden, PropagationClustering. from sknetwork.ranking import PageRank, Katz, HITS. from sknetwork.embedding import SVD, GSVD, Spectral. from sknetwork.path import shortest_path. from sknetwork.data import karate_club, house, star_wars. graph = sknetwork.data.karate_club(). adjacency = graph.adjacency. labels = Louvain().fit_predict(adjacency). scores = PageRank().fit_predict(adjacency). bipartite = graph.biadjacency.

gotchas: SciPy sparse matrix input required — no NetworkX graph objects directly
Bipartite graphs need biadjacency matrix, not adjacency
Louvain/Leiden results are stochastic without seed
Less feature-rich than NetworkX for general graph algorithms
best_when: Large-scale graph analysis with scikit-learn style API
PageRank, Katz centrality, HITS on large sparse graphs
Spectral embedding and SVD-based graph embedding
When SciPy sparse matrices are already available
avoid_when: Small graphs where NetworkX is more convenient
When NetworkX graph objects are the primary format
summary: scikit-network provides sklearn-style graph analysis on SciPy sparse matrices. Louvain/Leiden clustering. PageRank, Katz, HITS. SVD/Spectral embedding. Shortest paths. BSD-3 by Telecom Paris/Thomas Bonald. Best for large-scale graph analysis with sklearn-style API. SciPy sparse matrix input required. Not for deep learning GNNs. Stochastic algorithms need seed for reproducibility.
